confirmed_elements: Apache aff.conf points `/FireFlow/api` and `/aff/api` at local port 1989.
The local 1989 listener is attached to the aff-boot service family through descendant PID ownership.
This is enough to treat aff-boot as the current bounded local route owner for the AFF or FireFlow API edge.
boundary_packets.display_name: AFF or FireFlow 1989 Route Owner Packet
remaining_questions: The packet does not yet prove the full downstream FireFlow workflow behind aff-boot.
The packet does not yet replay requests or prove post-handoff business logic behavior.

route_evidence.match_reason: listener port 1989 matches exact PID ownership
listener port 1989 matches exact PID ownership
listener port 1989 matches exact PID ownership
listener port 1989 matches exact PID ownership
Apache config file family `aff.conf` matches component naming
Apache config file family `aff.conf` matches component naming

boundary_packets.why_it_matters: This packet keeps Apache route ownership, the local 1989 listener, and the owning service family in one bounded proof surface.

collection_policy.fact_inference_boundary: observed facts, tentative inference, and unknowns are kept separate
out_of_scope: log content interpretation beyond bounded marker checks
